data.description:
## Finding

Plan review catches child-list and child-fetch failures and can continue with an empty or partial hierarchy, allowing an ordinary review outcome despite degraded coverage.

## Evidence

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:8-25` catches child enumeration and fetch errors, substitutes an empty list or summary fallback, and continues.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:26-49` builds review context from the potentially incomplete child set.

## Remediation

Make hierarchy completeness explicit in the review context and verdict protocol. Retry recoverable reads. If the graph remains incomplete, return an explicit incomplete-context/indeterminate result and prohibit an ordinary clean pass unless the system can deterministically prove the missing context is irrelevant to the reviewed criteria.

Cascade flag: tightening failure behavior can affect claim-gate availability during transient tracker failures; preserve bounded retries and actionable diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Plan context records whether child enumeration and child detail loading completed successfully.
- [ ] Recoverable list/fetch failures receive bounded retries with actionable diagnostics.
- [ ] A remaining incomplete hierarchy cannot receive an ordinary clean pass.
- [ ] The only exception is a deterministic, tested proof that the omitted context is irrelevant to every criterion being decided.
- [ ] Explicit incomplete-context/indeterminate outcomes identify which hierarchy reads failed or were omitted.
- [ ] Tests cover total child-list failure, one-child detail failure, partial-summary fallback, retry recovery, and the relevance exception if implemented.
data.title: Prevent clean plan-review passes with incomplete hierarchy context
